objective: Make the first Today viewport feel like a credible local operating surface by turning the generic workflow rail into a Daily progress board with visible local evidence, next action, completion count, and step states.
riskClass: Q3 because this goal lane includes approved production deployment after local proof; app code itself is Q2 browser-local frontend state.
approvalStatus: Production promotion was previously approved by user message: PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_APPROVED=true promote. This slice does not edit credentials, auth, DNS, backend services, n8n, Telegram, Postgres, public MCP, or WHOOP settings.
truthBoundary: The Daily progress board derives only from browser-local demo state.
No WHOOP, account, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Postgres, backend write, reminder, timer, or cross-device sync readiness is claimed.
The separate LifeMax lab/source boot reports are red as of this run, so connected daily usability is not claimed.
acceptanceCriteria: /today has one clearly visible progress board above the deeper forms/editors.
The board shows exactly five anchors: Check-in, Plan, Capture, Close/review, Learn.
The header shows X/5 anchors complete and a concrete Current next action from the first incomplete anchor.
Evidence references browser-local artifacts only: check-in, saved plan, captures, close, archive/history.
State updates immediately after local actions and persists after refresh through existing localStorage behavior.
390px mobile and 1280px desktop have no horizontal overflow.
Copy avoids live integration, AI, backend, or medical readiness claims.
defectsFound: The previous five-tile rail did not summarize the full day loop at a product level; it looked like disconnected statuses rather than an operating board.

The first implementation counted Plan only when all plan items were done; planner critique clarified that the Plan anchor should complete when a plan is saved while its card still shows item progress.

The first Learn card used the generic experiment status 'ready', which could imply more readiness than local pattern evidence supports. It was changed to 'watching' unless a local experiment is active or a weekly board is ready.

Initial in-app browser smoke used two overly exact text assertions: one expected case-sensitive 'WATCHING' while the UI intentionally rendered 'watching', and one expected a capture label with exact line breaks. The smoke was repaired to verify the actual visible board evidence.
Standalone Playwright found two stale visual-spec assertions: Capture did not expose an aria label 'Captures: 3', and Profile's WHOOP boundary needed scoping to the Source status panel.
Standalone Playwright also repeatedly timed out launching Chromium before assertions on this Mac, matching earlier process-pressure failures.
repairsMade: Replaced the generic WorkflowRail rendering with DailyProgressBoard, preserving the existing derived state and localStorage path.
Added a daily-loop header with completion count, local percentage, current next action, evidence strip, next-review status, and learning status.
Changed Plan anchor completion to mean a browser-local plan exists, while the card still displays 0/3, 1/3, or 3/3 plan-item progress.
Renamed the close stage to Close/review and made missing inputs explicit when review is locked.
Changed Learn copy to 'watching', 'experiment', or 'weekly board' to avoid overclaiming readiness.
Added responsive CSS for a dense two-column mobile board, stable step cards, evidence strip, progress meter, and desktop layout.
Extended visual smoke coverage so the board must show 0/5, advance through local actions, show capture/close evidence, and persist after refresh.
Hardened stale Playwright assertions to match current Capture and Profile accessible surfaces.

flow: Reset browser-local demo data through /profile Reset local demo and Confirm reset.
Open /today and verify Daily progress board starts at 0/5 with Current: Save a manual check-in.
Save check-in and generate local plan, then verify 2/5 anchors and Current: Capture one useful local signal.
Open /capture, save a helped habit capture, return to /today, and verify 3/5 anchors with the capture label in the board.
Save evening close, save review checkpoint, save to history, and verify 4/5 anchors plus Close/review 1 archived.
Load sample week and verify 5/5 anchors, weekly board ready, and local-only evidence.
Open /privacy and verify Privacy policy and local data boundary is reachable.
Verify 390px mobile and 1280px desktop have scrollWidth equal to clientWidth.
Inspect console logs for serious errors.
stateTransitions: 0/5 anchors complete: no local evidence yet.
3/5 anchors complete: check-in, plan, one capture.
4/5 anchors complete: check-in, plan, capture, close/review archive.
5/5 anchors complete: sample week with check-in, plan, captures, close/review archives, and weekly board.

browserProduction.flow: reset local state -> /today board 0/5 -> save check-in and generated plan -> board 2/5 -> load sample week -> board 5/5 -> /privacy policy heading -> /today screenshot

plannerCritic.summary: Critic warned that the existing rail should be refined rather than rebuilt, plan completion semantics should not drift from local plan semantics, mobile density was fragile, and copy must avoid live readiness claims. The main thread incorporated the Plan anchor semantics, Close/review naming, cautious Learn copy, mobile layout constraints, and stronger board-specific verification.

independentVerifier.summary: Verifier inspected git state, proof packets, ledger, code/test surfaces, red connected-boundary artifacts, screenshots, and production URLs. It independently reran npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test, quality_gate.mjs, and jq JSON parsing; checked both production Today URLs returned HTTP 200 with Daily progress board and local-only boundary copy; and confirmed mobile/desktop screenshots show a visible, non-decorative board.
gaps: Full LifeMax completion remains blocked by red connected lab/source boot state; get_life_safety_state and get_grok_context_brief did not load in the fresh June 20 preflight.
Public app behavior is still browser-local demo behavior, not connected/shared-context LifeMax.
The verifier did not rerun the write-producing Playwright visual flow in read-only mode; it relied on stored screenshots, smoke packets, live HTML inspection, and non-writing checks.
independentVerifier.repairCompletedBeforeFinal: The verifier found a stale June 10 green Markdown preflight mirror beside the current red June 20 JSON. Main thread replaced /Users/oliverrussell/Desktop/LifeMax/ops/reports/latest-lifemax-local-agent-context-preflight.md with a red mirror that points to the current JSON as canonical.

residualRisks: This is still browser-local demo behavior; real backend/account/integration readiness is unverified and currently red in lab boot artifacts.
Standalone Playwright remains unreliable on this Mac because Chromium launch timed out before assertions; in-app browser proof was used for rendered UI verification.
The public app now has a stronger daily progress board, but the larger LifeMax goal is not complete because connected shared-context services remain red and unverified.
rollbackPath: Git rollback: revert the Daily progress board commit and redeploy the previous production commit if needed.
Runtime rollback: promote the previous Vercel production deployment. No database/auth/credential migration is part of this slice.
User-data rollback is not needed for backend records because this feature writes only browser-local demo state.
stopContinueDecision: checkpoint_not_complete: Daily progress board is committed, pushed, deployed, and smoked on both production aliases; larger connected LifeMax readiness remains red and unproved.
